科.origin: 禾 (grain) beside 斗 (a measuring scoop): sorting grain into kinds, so 科 means a category or branch of study like science.

sentence.en: I like science class.

秒.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 少 (small): a tiny slice of time, one second.

sentence.en: Wait one second for me.

租.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 且: long ago people paid rent in grain, so 租 means to rent.

sentence.en: We rented a car.

秦.origin: Sits over 禾 (grain). 秦 is the name of the Qin dynasty, which first united China.

sentence.en: The Qin dynasty is very famous.

积.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 只: piling up grain bit by bit, so 积 means to store up or accumulate.

sentence.en: He likes to play with building blocks.

移.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 多: moving plants to a new spot, so 移 means to move or shift.

sentence.en: Please move the chair a bit.

稀.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 希: when grain grows thinly spread, it is rare or sparse.

sentence.en: This kind of flower is very rare.

稍.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 肖: 稍 means a little or slightly.

sentence.en: The soup is slightly salty.

税.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 兑: grain handed over as payment to the state, so 税 means tax.

sentence.en: Everyone has to pay taxes.

稠.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 周: grain growing close together, so 稠 means dense or thick.

sentence.en: This bowl of porridge is very thick.

稳.origin: 禾 (grain) over a steady base: 稳 means steady and stable.

sentence.en: The table is very steady.

稻.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 舀: 稻 is the rice plant growing in the field.

sentence.en: Rice is planted in the field.

稿.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 高: 稿 means a draft or manuscript you write.

sentence.en: I am writing a draft.

穆.origin: 禾 (grain) on the left: 穆 means solemn and majestic, also a family surname.

sentence.en: The classroom is very solemn.

穗.origin: 禾 (grain) plus 惠: 穗 is the ear of grain at the top of the plant.

sentence.en: The ears of wheat have turned yellow.

穴.origin: Looks like the round mouth of a cave. 穴 means a cave or hole.

sentence.en: Little animals live in caves.

空.origin: 穴 (cave) over 工: an empty cave, so 空 means empty or sky.

sentence.en: The sky is very blue.

窃.origin: 穴 (hole) plus 切: sneaking through a hole, so 窃 means to steal.

sentence.en: Stealing is wrong.

窑.origin: 穴 (cave) over 缶 (pot): a cave-like oven where pots are fired, a kiln.

sentence.en: Porcelain is fired in the kiln.

窖.origin: 穴 (cave) plus 告: 窖 is a cellar dug into the ground for storage.

sentence.en: The potatoes are kept in the cellar.

窗.origin: 穴 (hole) over 囱: an opening in the wall, so 窗 is a window.

sentence.en: Please open the window.

窘.origin: 穴 (cave) plus 君: feeling trapped, so 窘 means awkward or embarrassed.

sentence.en: He was so embarrassed his face turned red.

窜.origin: 穴 (hole) over 串: a mouse darting out of its hole, so 窜 means to scurry or flee.

sentence.en: The mouse scurried everywhere.

窝.origin: 穴 (cave) plus 呙: a small hollow home, so 窝 means a nest or den.

sentence.en: There is a bird's nest in the tree.

窟.origin: 穴 (cave) plus 屈: 窟 means a hole or cave.

sentence.en: We visited the rock caves.

竖.origin: A person standing 立 straight: 竖 means vertical or to stand upright.

sentence.en: Please write a vertical line.

竞.origin: 立 (stand) over 兄: standing up to a rival, so 竞 means to compete.

sentence.en: I took part in the competition.

竟.origin: 音 (sound) over 儿: a song finished at last, so 竟 means finally or unexpectedly.

sentence.en: He unexpectedly won.

笋.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 尹: 笋 is the young bamboo shoot you can eat.

sentence.en: Pandas love to eat bamboo shoots.

笔.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 毛 (hair): a bamboo rod tipped with hair makes a writing brush, a pen.

sentence.en: I write with a pencil.

笙.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 生: 笙 is a reed mouth organ made of bamboo pipes.

sentence.en: He can play the sheng.

笛.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 由: 笛 is the bamboo flute.

sentence.en: She is playing the flute.

符.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 付: tokens were once split bamboo tallies, so 符 means a sign, mark, or charm.

sentence.en: This symbol is a question mark.

笨.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 本: 笨 means clumsy or stupid.

sentence.en: The little bear is a bit clumsy.

筋.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 肋 (flesh and strength): tough bamboo-like fibers in the body, so 筋 means muscles or tendons.

sentence.en: Exercise strengthens muscles and bones.

筐.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 匡: 筐 is a basket woven from bamboo.

sentence.en: The basket is full of apples.

筒.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 同: a hollow bamboo joint, so 筒 means a tube or cylinder.

sentence.en: The pens are in the pen holder.

策.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 朿: a bamboo riding whip; also a written plan or strategy.

sentence.en: We thought of a good countermeasure.

筛.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 师: a bamboo mesh for sifting, so 筛 means a sieve or to sift.

sentence.en: Use a sieve to sift the flour.

筹.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 寿: bamboo counting sticks, so 筹 means a chip or to plan.

sentence.en: We are preparing for the party.

签.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 佥: a slip of bamboo for marking; also to sign your name.

sentence.en: Please sign your name here.

简.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 间: old books were bamboo slips, so 简 means simple or a letter.

sentence.en: This problem is very simple.

箍.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) plus 㧜: bamboo bands wrapped tight, so 箍 means a hoop or to bind.

sentence.en: There is an iron hoop around the wooden barrel.

箩.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 罗: 箩 is a large bamboo basket.

sentence.en: The basket holds rice.

箫.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 肃: 箫 is a vertical bamboo flute.

sentence.en: Grandpa can play the xiao flute.

箭.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 前: arrows were made of bamboo, so 箭 means arrow.

sentence.en: He shot an arrow.

箱.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 相: a bamboo case, so 箱 means a box or chest.

sentence.en: The clothes are in the box.

篓.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 娄: 篓 is a tall bamboo basket.

sentence.en: The fish were put into the bamboo basket.

篦.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 囟 and 比: a fine bamboo comb, so 篦 means a fine-toothed comb.

sentence.en: She combs her hair with a fine comb.

篮.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 监: a woven bamboo basket, so 篮 means basket, as in basketball.

sentence.en: We play basketball together.
